discovery.executive-summary: Public dashboard tracking 4 market-priced signals for the Hormuz strait crisis. Built for fund managers who need signal over noise. Ships today.

problem-space.core-problem: Information environment around the Hormuz crisis is garbage. Trump says war ends tomorrow, Iran says Hormuz shut forever. Analysts range $60-$150. Can't build a portfolio view on noise.
problem-space.context: India imports 85% of crude. Half normally passes through Hormuz. User runs India-focused equity fund — crisis directly affects portfolio.
problem-space.insight: 4 signals are priced by people with real money on the line. They don't lie. Everything else is noise.

signal.name: Ship Insurance Premiums
signal.description: Lloyd's underwriter war risk premium as % of hull value
signal.data-source: HormuzTracker aggregation + manual broker quotes from Lloyd's List
signal.rationale: Underwriters have billions at stake on every pricing call. No press conference replicates this.

signal.name: AIS Ship Transit Count
signal.description: Daily vessel crossings through Hormuz via AIS GPS tracking
signal.data-source: WTO Hormuz Trade Tracker, free AIS data sources
signal.rationale: You can't spin a ship being somewhere it isn't.

nuances.nuance: Return legs (ships heading BACK to reload) are the real leading indicator. 4-5 trips/month needed for India.
AIS switching off is normal in risky zones — absolute count is unreliable.
AIS accuracy itself is the signal. When operators feel safe enough to keep AIS on, risk environment is normalizing. Until then, dark fleet = elevated risk.

signal.name: Paper vs Physical Oil Spread
signal.description: Gap between Brent crude (paper/futures) and Dubai physical (what Asian buyers actually pay)
signal.threshold: Spread narrowing = paper catching up to reality
signal.data-source: Free oil price APIs for Brent, Dubai physical from commodity data feeds
signal.rationale: Trump's jawboning pushes paper prices down. Refiners buying cargo get no discount. If you watch Brent for India's oil bill, you're watching the wrong number.

signal.name: Mid-April Supply Cliff
signal.description: Multiple emergency measures expiring simultaneously around April 15

events.event: US waiver for India buying Russian crude expires

signal.projected-gap: ~10 mb/d without stopgaps (BCA Research) — largest crude disruption ever
signal.threshold: If Hormuz doesn't reopen by mid-April = uncharted territory

persona.need: Actionable signals to assess India oil exposure without drowning in headlines
persona.current-solution: Manual tracking across multiple sources, news reading

persona.role: Commodity traders, energy analysts, macro strategists
persona.need: Real-time crisis dashboard for Hormuz shipping disruption

technical-decisions.data-strategy: Free tier: oil price APIs + free AIS sources + HormuzTracker scraping + manual admin input for insurance quotes
technical-decisions.alerts: Dashboard only — visual threshold indicators, no push notifications

business-model.monetization: None — credibility/utility tool for the fund
business-model.success-metric: Dashboard live and usable before mid-April cliff

risks.risk: Free AIS data may be delayed or incomplete (dark fleet problem)
Insurance premium data requires manual updates — no public API
Dubai physical price may not be available from free APIs — may need proxy
Hormuz crisis resolves quickly making tracker less relevant

next-steps.step: Skip market analysis — problem is validated, urgency is clear
Fast-track to genius-architect for stack decisions and task breakdown
Ship MVP today with hardcoded/manual data where APIs unavailable
